# Device Power Alerts

> Monitor battery voltage, battery level percentage, and external power supply state transmitted by GPS tracking hardware, and emit alerts when power conditions threaten continuous device operation (low battery, power cut, power restoration).

**Category:** Notification · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · battery · power · alert · fleet · hardware

## What this does

Monitor battery voltage, battery level percentage, and external power supply state transmitted by GPS tracking hardware, and emit alerts when power conditions threaten continuous device operation (low battery, power cut, power restoration).

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **battery_level** *(number, optional)*
- **battery_voltage** *(number, optional)*
- **external_power_voltage** *(number, optional)*
- **alarm** *(text, optional)*

## What must be true

- **0:** Power alarm codes are embedded in the position alarm attribute by the device; each code generates a separate alert event
- **1:** low_battery indicates the internal battery is below a device-defined threshold; devices typically decide the threshold in firmware
- **2:** power_cut indicates the vehicle external power supply has been disconnected (possible tampering or vehicle power failure)
- **3:** power_restored indicates external power has been reconnected after a power_cut event
- **4:** power_off and power_on reflect transitions detected by the device in the main power rail
- **5:** Battery and power voltage readings are stored on every position and can be charted over time to identify degradation trends
- **6:** Power alert events follow the same alarm processing pipeline as other alarm types (see device-alarm-notifications); duplicate suppression applies

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **No Power Data** — when position does not include any power or battery attributes, then No power events generated for this position; device does not report power telemetry.
- **Battery Data Recorded** — when position includes battery_level or battery_voltage attributes, then Battery telemetry captured on position record; available for trend analysis and reporting.
- **Power Alarm Generated** — when position alarm attribute contains a power-related alarm code; duplicate suppression is disabled OR alarm code was not present in the previous position, then Power alert event stored; notification handlers dispatch alerts to subscribed users.

## Errors it can return

- `POWER_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` — The device referenced does not exist

## Connects to

- **device-alarm-notifications**
- **gps-position-ingestion**
- **device-status-tracking**
